title: BehaviorEvent Schema
description: Schema for internal behavioral events captured by the eBPF tracing daemon.

header.description: Correlation metadata shared across all layers.

timestamp.description: Event timestamp in seconds since epoch (float for precision).

auid.description: Audit User ID (Login UID). Remains constant across privilege escalations.

sessionid.description: Audit Session ID assigned by the kernel at login.

pid.description: Process ID that triggered the event.

comm.description: The command name of the process.

type.description: The source/nature of the event. LIFECYCLE events are userspace-synthesised process-lifecycle signals (process_start, process_fork, process_exit). HEARTBEAT events are periodic userspace-synthesised pulses carrying drop deltas for undecidable-interval detection.

name.description: Specific name of the hook point (e.g., 'openat', 'tty_write').

layer.description: The abstraction layer this event belongs to.

proc.description: Process context at the time of the event.

main_executable.description: Absolute path to the binary being executed.

cwd.description: Current working directory of the process.

args.description: Extracted arguments or payload of the event. Fields vary by event type.

data.description: Base64-encoded raw bytes (used for TTY input/output).

fd_type.description: File descriptor type classification (used for read/write events).

syscall_nr.description: Syscall number (used for SYSCALL type Tier 1 raw events).

raw_args.description: Raw syscall arguments as integers (used for SYSCALL type Tier 1 raw events).

return_code.description: Exit code or return value of the syscall/operation.
